v2_sources.title: Tislelizumab Plus Platinum and Etoposide Versus Placebo Plus Platinum and Etoposide as First-Line Treatment for Extensive-Stage SCLC (RATIONALE-312): A Multicenter, Double-Blind, Placebo-Controlled, Randomized, Phase 3 Clinical Trial
v2_sources.citation: Tislelizumab Plus Platinum and Etoposide Versus Placebo Plus Platinum and Etoposide as First-Line Treatment for Extensive-Stage SCLC (RATIONALE-312): A Multicenter, Double-Blind, Placebo-Controlled, Randomized, Phase 3 Clinical Trial. Journal of Thoracic Oncology. 2024.

boundary_tags.population_or_model: Patients with extensive-stage small cell lung cancer (ES-SCLC) receiving first-line treatment

v2_facts.definition: {'content': 'Tislelizumab plus chemotherapy exhibited a statistically significant OS benefit versus placebo plus chemotherapy (stratified hazard ratio = 0.75 [95% CI: 0.61-0.93]; one-sided p = 0.0040)
v2_facts.definition_zh: {'content': 'Tislelizumab plus chemotherapy exhibited a statistically significant OS benefit versus placebo plus chemotherapy (stratified hazard ratio = 0.75 [95% CI: 0.61-0.93]; one-sided p = 0.0040)

v2_facts.definition: {'content': 'Median overall survival was 15.5 months (95% CI: 13.5-17.1) in the tislelizumab arm versus 13.5 months (95% CI: 12.1-14.9) in the placebo arm', 'page': 5}
v2_facts.definition_zh: {'content': 'Median overall survival was 15.5 months (95% CI: 13.5-17.1) in the tislelizumab arm versus 13.5 months (95% CI: 12.1-14.9) in the placebo arm', 'page': 5}

v2_facts.definition: {'content': 'Estimated 1-, 2-, and 3-year OS rates in the tislelizumab versus placebo arms were 63% versus 58%, 33% versus 22%, and 25% versus 9%, respectively', 'page': 5}
v2_facts.definition_zh: {'content': 'Estimated 1-, 2-, and 3-year OS rates in the tislelizumab versus placebo arms were 63% versus 58%, 33% versus 22%, and 25% versus 9%, respectively', 'page': 5}

v2_facts.definition: {'content': 'Progression-free survival was significantly improved in the tislelizumab versus placebo arm (stratified hazard ratio = 0.64 [95% CI: 0.52-0.78]; p < 0.0001)', 'page': 2}
v2_facts.definition_zh: {'content': 'Progression-free survival was significantly improved in the tislelizumab versus placebo arm (stratified hazard ratio = 0.64 [95% CI: 0.52-0.78]; p < 0.0001)', 'page': 2}

v2_facts.definition: {'content': 'Median PFS was 4.7 months (95% CI: 4.3-5.5) in the tislelizumab arm and 4.3 months (95% CI: 4.2-4.4) in the placebo arm', 'page': 6}
v2_facts.definition_zh: {'content': 'Median PFS was 4.7 months (95% CI: 4.3-5.5) in the tislelizumab arm and 4.3 months (95% CI: 4.2-4.4) in the placebo arm', 'page': 6}

v2_facts.definition: {'content': 'Confirmed objective response rate was 68% (95% CI: 62-74) in the tislelizumab arm versus 62% (95% CI: 55-68) in the placebo arm', 'page': 6}
v2_facts.definition_zh: {'content': 'Confirmed objective response rate was 68% (95% CI: 62-74) in the tislelizumab arm versus 62% (95% CI: 55-68) in the placebo arm', 'page': 6}

v2_facts.definition: {'content': 'Grade ≥3 treatment-related adverse events were reported in 86% of patients in each treatment arm and were mostly hematologic events including neutropenia (56% vs 55%) and decreased white
v2_facts.definition_zh: {'content': 'Grade ≥3 treatment-related adverse events were reported in 86% of patients in each treatment arm and were mostly hematologic events including neutropenia (56% vs 55%) and decreased white

v2_facts.definition: {'content': 'Immune-mediated adverse events of any grade were reported in 38% of patients in the tislelizumab arm versus 18% in the placebo arm, with hypothyroidism (14% vs 4%), rash (13% vs 8%), and
v2_facts.definition_zh: {'content': 'Immune-mediated adverse events of any grade were reported in 38% of patients in the tislelizumab arm versus 18% in the placebo arm, with hypothyroidism (14% vs 4%), rash (13% vs 8%), and
